summary: next-locomotive-init exfiltrates installer identity on postinstall
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle script runs automatically on `npm install` and collects the installer's OS username (os.userInfo().username) and hostname (os.hostname()), then POSTs them to a hardcoded non-first-party endpoint at https://seedance2-api.vercel.app/api/collect. The transmitted value is formatted as `${username}@${hostname}`. The package advertises itself as animation utilities for Next.js templates — a purpose that has no requirement for install-time network I/O — and the beacon is undisclosed with no opt-out. This is install-time reconnaissance/exfiltration of installer identifiers to an author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
